meta.source_list: Meta Ad Library, TikTok Creative Center, AppMagic, Sensor Tower, SimilarWeb

format_insight.headline: UGC and transformation content dominate — but most apps ignore 12+ formats.
format_insight.body: 78% of observed creatives fall into just 6 format categories. That leaves 12 format types with minimal competition — a clear differentiation opportunity for brands willing to diversify.

emotional_insight.headline: Frustration and aspiration drive 65% of all hooks.
emotional_insight.body: The most common emotional triggers cluster around two themes: frustration with current results and aspiration toward a transformed self. Brands that layer in trust-building and urgency triggers see higher completion rates.

generation_insight.headline: Format + headline + trigger = a complete brief for AI generation.
generation_insight.body: Each combination of format type, headline template, and emotional trigger produces a unique creative brief. With 18 formats x 15 templates x 10 triggers, the addressable creative space is 2,700 unique combinations — most of which are untested by any competitor.

tiers.label: Dominant
tiers.formats: UGC, Before-After, Talking Head, App Demo

tiers.label: Growing
tiers.formats: Tutorial, Influencer, Storytelling, Meme, Comparison, Podcast

tiers.label: Underexplored
tiers.formats: News, Case Study, AI-Generated, Overlay, BTS, Conference, Explainer, Carousel

headlines.template: If you're trying to [goal], do NOT [mistake]
Stop doing this if you want [result]
What no one tells you about [topic]

emotional_triggers.trigger: Frustrated with lack of visible results despite effort
Desire to look and feel confident in their body
Fear of wasting time on the wrong workout plan
Guilt about inconsistency or falling off track
Time scarcity — need fast, efficient solutions
Skepticism about app-based fitness vs. personal trainers
Health scare or doctor's recommendation as catalyst

emotional_categories.description: Anger at lack of progress, wasted effort
Vision of ideal self, transformation desire
Fear of wrong choices, wasted time

example_triggers: I've been working out for 3 months and I look exactly the same
Every influencer says something different — I don't know who to trust
I don't have 2 hours a day to spend at the gym
My friend lost 20 lbs in 8 weeks and I can't even lose 5
I keep starting and stopping — I need something that actually sticks

direct.name: FitPulse
direct.gap_assessment: High volume but low format diversity — concentrated in 4 formats

direct.name: SweatZone
direct.gap_assessment: Strong influencer pipeline but no data-driven or comparison content

direct.name: GymBuddy
direct.gap_assessment: Product-focused creatives — missing emotional hooks entirely

direct.name: BodyForge
direct.gap_assessment: Meme-heavy strategy — high engagement but unclear conversion path

indirect.name: NutriFuel
indirect.relevance: Nutrition app — overlapping audience, different entry point. Their UGC production quality sets the benchmark.

indirect.name: MindCalm
indirect.relevance: Wellness/meditation — competing for the same 'self-improvement' budget. Podcast-style ads performing well.

indirect.name: RunTrack Pro
indirect.relevance: Running-specific — adjacent category. Their data-driven creative approach is underused in broader fitness.

emerging.name: FlexAI
emerging.growth_signal: 3x creative volume increase in last 60 days

emerging.name: PulseUp
emerging.growth_signal: New market entrant — 5x spend increase month-over-month

a.label: Test
a.best_for: Lowest risk. Validate the model before committing.

b.label: Scale
b.best_for: Best balance of volume and efficiency. Recommended starting point.

c.label: Dominate
c.best_for: Maximum competitive moat. Hard for competitors to replicate.

decision_framework.why: Highest testing velocity, winners found fastest
Covers all formats, templates, and triggers

roadmap.milestones: First creative batches live, initial format tests, top 3 headline templates in rotation, first CPA data points
Format coverage expands to 12, emotional trigger testing begins, winning formats identified and doubled down
Full format coverage, all headline templates active, CPA improvement measurable, competitive volume parity
Refresh winning creatives, test Tier 3 formats, maximize coverage of emotional triggers, full creative system running

levers.title: Format Diversification
levers.text: Expand from the 4 dominant formats into 12+ categories. Most competitors cluster in UGC and Before-After — diversifying into Podcast-Style, Data-Driven, and Comparison content reaches untapped audience segments.

levers.title: Headline Template Rotation
levers.text: Systematically rotate through all 15 proven headline structures. Most ads use 3-4 templates on repeat. Broader rotation prevents fatigue and discovers unexpected winners.

levers.title: Emotional Trigger Coverage
levers.text: Map each creative to a specific emotional trigger. Current market over-indexes on Frustration and Aspiration. Under-leveraged triggers like Trust Deficit and Urgency have lower competition.

levers.title: Template Content at Scale
levers.text: Use the format x headline x trigger matrix to generate unique briefs. 18 formats x 15 templates x 10 triggers = 2,700 possible combinations. Test 5-10% monthly for systematic coverage.

levers.title: Creative Refresh Cadence
levers.text: Replace bottom 20% of creatives every 2 weeks. Top performers run until fatigue signals appear in CPA data. This keeps the portfolio fresh without losing proven winners.
